Run this integration as part of your initial setup of Vulnerability Response and prior to importing vulnerability data into your instance with a third-party scanner product.
          - [Preparing for the NVD integrations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/nvd-prerequisites.md) -- A successful integration requires planning and careful execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The NVD integrations assume that you are familiar with the NIST National Vulnerability Database (NVD).
          - [Configure and run the scheduled job for updating CWE records](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/t_ConfigureScheduledJobsCWE.md) -- Data imports from the CWE further enrich the vulnerability data in your instance. Use Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) records downloaded from the CWE database for reference when deciding whether a vulnerability must be escalated. Run this integration as part of your initial setup of Vulnerability Response and prior to importing vulnerability data into your instance with a third-party scanner product.
          - [Install the Vulnerability Response Integration with the NIST National Vulnerability Database](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/install-nvd.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the NIST National Vulnerability Database (NVD) product properly integrates with Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
            - [Activate the NIST National Vulnerability Database–API (Unmapped CPE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/install-nvd.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the NIST National Vulnerability Database (NVD) product properly integrates with Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
            - [Activate the NIST National Vulnerability Database–API (CPE only)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/install-nvd.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the NIST National Vulnerability Database (NVD) product properly integrates with Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
          - [Optional NVD integration modification and activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/nvd-optional-modifications.md) -- Configure optional modifications specifically for the NVD integrations.
            - [Perform a manual NVD integration import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/manual-nvd-import.md) -- If your initial import failed, or you don’t want to wait for the scheduled initial import, you can perform a full data import independent of the daily or weekly scheduled job.
          - [View the (National Vulnerability Database) NVD integration import run status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/nvd-check-imports.md) -- Use the Vulnerability Integration Runs related list to verify the success of your integration runs, locate any issues, and inform your remediation decisions.
          - [Add CVEs to third-party entries](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/add-cve2thirdparty-entry.md) -- Common Vulnerability and Exposure (CVE) information can be related to a single third-party vulnerability. It is usually added during a third-party integration import however, you can add multiple CVEs (vulnerabilities) manually.
          - [View Vulnerability Response vulnerability libraries](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-vuln-libraries.md) -- You can view vulnerability data imported from the National Vulnerability Database (NVD), Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE), or third-parties to decide whether to escalate a remediation task.
        - [Central Vulnerability Database](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cvd-overview.md) -- The Central Vulnerability Database (CVDB) is a source-agnostic vulnerability data repository that consolidates and enriches vulnerability records from multiple security sources into a single, authoritative view. Use CVDB to eliminate conflicting data across your vulnerability integrations and gain full visibility into which source is authoritative for each field.
          - [Integrations for Central Vulnerability Database](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cvd-integrations-overview.md) -- The Central Vulnerability Database supports integration with trusted global vulnerability data sources, including the National Vulnerability Database, European Union Vulnerability Database (EUVD), and Japanese Vulnerability Notes (JVN), to enrich and normalize vulnerability records.
            - [Central Vulnerability Database: versions and dependencies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cvd-versions-and-dependencies.md) -- Lists the available release versions and application dependencies required to run the Integrations for Central Vulnerability Database.
            - [Activate the ENISA EUVD integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cvd-activate-enisa-euvd-integration.md) -- Activate the ENISA EUVD integration to ingest vulnerability data from the European Union Vulnerability Database (EUVD) into your ServiceNow instance.
            - [Activate the Japanese Vulnerability Notes Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cvd-activate-jvn-integration.md) -- Activate the JVN Integration to ingest vulnerability data from the Japanese Vulnerability Notes (JVN) database using scheduled or on-demand integration runs.
            - [View the Integrations for Central Vulnerability Database Import Run Status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-cvd-integration-run-status.md) -- Use the Vulnerability Integration Runs related list to verify the success of your integration runs, identify issues, and inform remediation decisions.
            - [View Central Vulnerability Database vulnerability data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-cvd-vulnerability-data.md) -- View vulnerability records imported through the EUVD and JVN integrations in the NVD Entry table within your ServiceNow instance.
            - [Roles and permissions for Integrations for Central Vulnerability Database](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cvd-roles-and-permissions.md) -- Roles required to view, configure, and execute the EUVD and JVN integrations and to access related vulnerability data.
        - [CISA Known Exploit Vulnerability (KEV) Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cisa-vuln-integration.md) -- The Vulnerability Response integration with the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEVs) catalog ingests data to help you effectively prioritize and remediate these vulnerabilities.
          - [Preparing for the CISA integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cisa-prerequisites.md) -- A successful integration requires planning and careful execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The CISA Known Exploited Vulnerability integration assumes that you are familiar with the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).
          - [Install the ServiceNow Vulnerability Response Integration with CISA application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/install-cisa.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Known Exploited Vulnerability advisor integrates with Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
        - [Understanding the Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/epss-vr-integration-overview.md) -- Overview of the EPSS integration with Vulnerability Response.
          - [Configure and run a scheduled job to update CVE records with EPSS data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/config-schedule-job-update-cve-record-epss-data.md) -- Customize the base system scheduled job to update CVE records with EPSS data.
          - [Add EPSS Score condition in Risk calculator Business Rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/add-epss-score-condition-risk-calc-business-rule.md) -- Modify Business Rule to add EPSS score condition to Risk Calculator.
        - [Microsoft Defender Integration for Security Exposure Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ms-defender-sem.md) -- The Microsoft Defender Integration for Security Exposure Management plugin provides a unified integration point for importing security data from Microsoft Defender into your ServiceNow instance. Use this plugin to configure and manage two integrations from a single application.
          - [Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-integration.md) -- The Vulnerability Response integration with Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) application uses data imported from MS TVM to help you prioritize and remediate vulnerabilities for your assets. The application is available with a separate subscription from the ServiceNow Store.
            - [Preparing for the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-prep-checklist.md) -- You can prepare for the ServiceNow Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) Vulnerability integration by performing setup tasks.
            - [Set up Microsoft Azure for the MS TVM integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-azure-account.md) -- Set up your account in the Microsoft Azure portal to access the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) API remotely. You need this account so that you can access the MS TVM tenant to gather information for machines, vulnerabilities, and security recommendations.
            - [Install and configure the Vulnerability Response Integration with the MS TVM application using Setup Assistant](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-install-and-configure.md) -- Install and configure the Vulnerability Response integration with the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) application so that you can use the data that is imported from MS TVM to prioritize and remediate vulnerabilities for your assets.
            - [Data retrieval settings for the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-data-rerieve.md) -- You can use data retrieval settings to help you determine the type and scope of data that you want to import from the ServiceNow Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) Vulnerability integration to your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
            - [REST messages for the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-rest-msgs.md) -- You can use the REST messages for the ServiceNow Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) Vulnerability Integration to make calls to the MS TVM API.
              - [Data transformation for the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-data-transformref.md) -- After you identify the data that you want to import, the data is retrieved from the ServiceNow Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) application, processed through a set of data sources, and transformed in your instance.
            - [Integration run status chart for the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-integration-run.md) -- You can use the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) Integration Run Status module to provide you with a graphical view of the status of the MS TVM integration runs.
              - [Verify the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management integration import run status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-view-import-run-status.md) -- Use the Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) import run status to verify the success of your integration runs and to locate any issues.
            - [Split Microsoft TVM detections based on the vulnerability instance to split vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-split-detections.md) -- ServiceNow Vulnerability Response enables the splitting of detections from Microsoft Threat and Vulnerability Management (MS TVM) scanners, enabling the creation of a unique vulnerable item (VIT) for each detected vulnerability instance. This split enables the assignment of VITs to various remediation teams, enhancing the management and tracking of vulnerabilities.
          - [Microsoft Defender](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cc_asc_overview.md) -- The Microsoft Defender for Cloud Integration product is an infrastructure security management system that enhances the security posture of your cloud environments.
            - [Migrate from Microsoft Defender for Cloud Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mstvm-migrate.md) -- If you're upgrading from the standalone Microsoft Defender for Cloud Integration application, follow these steps to migrate your existing data to the unified Microsoft Defender Integration for Security Exposure Management plugin.
            - [Install and configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cc_asc_install_configure.md) -- Install and configure the Microsoft Defender for Cloud Integration for Security Operations, so that you can use the data that is imported from Microsoft Defender for Cloud to prioritize and remediate any misconfigurations on your assets.
            - [Integration imports](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cc-asc-policies.md) -- Configuration Compliance imports policies, tests, authoritative sources, and test results from third-party integrations and stores them in modules for viewing.
        - [Understanding the HCL BigFix patch orchestration integration with Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-integration.md) -- You can manage patches and patch deployments for critical vulnerabilities for large groups of assets with the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with the HCL BigFix product.
          - [Integrations and dependencies of the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration integration with HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-integration-list.md) -- The following product and dependency applications are required for the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with HCL BigFix Integration. These applications are available in the ServiceNow Store.
          - [Prepare for the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration integration with HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-setup-checklist.md) -- Prepare for the integration by performing the following setup tasks.
          - [Install the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with HCL BigFix application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfixinstall.md) -- Download the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with HCL BigFix application from the ServiceNow Store.
          - [Configure the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-config.md) -- After you have installed the application, configure it with your BigFix account information and validate your credentials.
          - [Viewing patch data for the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-rollup.md) -- Patch data and patch rollup data, as well as vulnerability information and remediation status of your vulnerabilities, are displayed on records in your instance.
          - [Schedule patches with the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-schedule-patch.md) -- Schedule patches from Patch Update and Remediation task records in the Vulnerability Response application in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [REST messages for the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-REST-msg.md) -- The Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with HCL BigFix REST messages are used to make calls to the BigFix API.
          - [Data transformation for the patch orchestration integration with HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-data-transform.md) -- Data is retrieved from the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration with HCL BigFix integration, processed through a set of data sources, and transformed in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [Example workflow for the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with HCL BigFix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-bigfix-use-case.md) -- An example of how patch orchestration works with the HCL BigFix product.
        - [Understanding the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with Microsoft SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mspatch-integration.md) -- Manage patches and patch deployments for the critical vulnerabilities on your assets with the Vulnerability Response integration with the Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) product.
          - [Integrations and dependencies of the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with the Microsoft SCCM application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-integration-list.md) -- The following product and dependency applications are required for the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) integration. These applications are available in the ServiceNow Store
          - [Prepare for the Vulnerability Response patch orchestration integration with Microsoft SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-setup-checklist.md) -- Prepare for the integration by performing the following setup tasks.
          - [Install the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with Microsoft SCCM application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-install.md) -- Download the application from the ServiceNow Store.
          - [Configure the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with Microsoft SCCM integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-config.md) -- After you have installed the application, configure it with your account information and validate your credentials.
          - [Viewing patch data and patch data rollup for the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration integration with Microsoft SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-rollup.md) -- Patch data and patch rollup data, as well as vulnerability information and remediation status of your vulnerabilities are displayed on records in your instance.
          - [Schedule patches with the Microsoft SCCM integration with Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-schedule-patch.md) -- Schedule patches from Patch Update and Remediation task records in the Vulnerability Response application in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [REST messages for the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with Microsoft SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-REST-msg.md) -- The Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration with Microsoft SCCM uses the SCCM Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) classes to make calls to the SCCM Server API.
          - [Data transformation for the Patch Orchestration with Microsoft SCCM Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-data-transform.md) -- Data is retrieved from the Patch Orchestration with Microsoft SCCM Integration, processed through a set of data sources, and transformed in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [Example workflow for the Vulnerability Response Patch Orchestration integration with Microsoft SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-mspatch-use-case.md) -- An example of how patch orchestration works with Microsoft SCCM.
        - [Understanding the Qualys Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_QualysVulnIntegration.md) -- The Qualys product sensors collect the data and automatically send it to the Qualys application, which continuously analyzes and correlates the information. It easily integrates with Vulnerability Response as the Qualys Vulnerability Integration to map vulnerabilities to CIs and business services to determine impact and priority of potentially malicious threats.
          - [Preparing for the Qualys Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-prerequisites.md) -- A successful integration requires planning and careful execution of pre-integration tasks. It is essential that you prepare for the integration by performing these procedures. The Qualys Vulnerability Integration assumes that you are familiar with and run Qualys Cloud Platform scans in your environment.
          - [Install the Qualys Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/install-and-configure-qualys.md) -- Before you run the Qualys Vulnerability Integration in your instance, you must install and configure the Qualys Vulnerability Integration application. This application is available as a separate subscription.
            - [Activate the Qualys scanners](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/activate-qualys-scanners.md) -- You must activate the scanners after installing the Qualys Vulnerability Integration because the scanners are deactivated by default in the Vulnerability Response application.
            - [Installed components](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-installed-with.md) -- The following roles, integration jobs, and tables are installed with the Qualys Vulnerability Integration.
          - [Update configuration items with the network partition identifier for the Qualys Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-updateCI-NPI.md) -- Create unique configuration items (CIs) for assets in your environment that share IP addresses. Identify the distinct assets across your environment and automatically update the CIs on your existing discovered item, vulnerable item, and detection records to give you more details about your vulnerabilities.
          - [Optional Qualys modifications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/optional-modifications.md) -- Configure optional modifications and streamline some of the data specifically for the Qualys integration.

            - [Modify an initial start date](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/optional-modifications.md) -- Configure optional modifications and streamline some of the data specifically for the Qualys integration.

Most of these modifications require coding or advanced ServiceNow or Qualys Cloud Platform expertise.
              - [Enable base system filter for confirmed detection imports](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/advanced-modifications.md) -- Configure advanced optional modifications and streamline some of the data specifically for the Qualys integration. Most of these modifications require coding or advanced ServiceNow or Qualys Cloud Platform expertise.
              - [Initiate rescan for the Qualys Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/advanced-modifications.md) -- Configure advanced optional modifications and streamline some of the data specifically for the Qualys integration. Most of these modifications require coding or advanced ServiceNow or Qualys Cloud Platform expertise.
          - [Qualys metadata for vulnerabilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-kernal-service-metadata.md) -- The Qualys Host Detection Integration is enhanced to include more fields from Qualys. These fields help in filtering the non-exploitable Linux vulnerabilities. This enhancement improves the integration at the detection level and at the vulnerable item (VI) level.
            - [Import additional metadata from Qualys](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/import-additional-metadata.md) -- Import additional metadata for vulnerability items (VI) to filter vulnerabilities based on the impact on a kernel or service.
            - [Qualys metadata values for vulnerabilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-metadata-values.md) -- When the kernel metadata, service metadata, and configuration metadata are imported, some values are also retrieved. The values 0, 1 and Not available are processed in the back-end and converted to No, Yes and Empty respectively.
            - [Set additional filter parameters for Qualys imports](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-add-filters.md) -- Customize the filtering parameters for your scheduled queries with the Qualys Vulnerability Integration to help you further refine the vulnerability data you import with the supported Qualys integrations.
          - [Resolving Qualys Vulnerability Integration issues](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-troubleshooting.md) -- Some commonly encountered issues, along with workarounds are discussed.
            - [Attachments not appearing after import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-troubleshooting.md) -- Some commonly encountered issues, along with workarounds are discussed.
            - [Modify transform maps](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-troubleshooting.md) -- Some commonly encountered issues, along with workarounds are discussed.
            - [Check XML attachment property size](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-troubleshooting.md) -- Some commonly encountered issues, along with workarounds are discussed.
            - [Data retrieval limitations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-troubleshooting.md) -- Some commonly encountered issues, along with workarounds are discussed.
            - [Resolving Qualys Knowledge Base Integration failure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-troubleshooting.md) -- Some commonly encountered issues, along with workarounds are discussed.
          - [Qualys Vulnerability Integration reporting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_QualysHomepage.md) -- The Qualys Cloud Platform overview is an executive view into vulnerability activity. By providing trends, reports, and drill-downs into specific data, an administrator or analyst can quickly pinpoint areas of concern. The charts are populated with data after vulnerable items and the Qualys knowledge base data have been retrieved.
            - [Qualys integration run status chart](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-integr-run-status.md) -- The Qualys Integration Run Status module is a graphical view of the status of Qualys integration runs.
          - [Qualys data transformation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_QualysDataTransformation.md) -- The data retrieved from Qualys is processed through a set of data sources and transforms.
            - [Dynamic Search List Import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-dyn-srch-list-transform.md) -- The Qualys dynamic search list transform map is used to transform and import Qualys Dynamic Search Lists. Changes to this transform alter how Dynamic Search Lists are processed and inserted into the system.
            - [Static Search List Import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-static-srch-list-import.md) -- The Qualys static search list transform map is used to transform and import Qualys Static Search Lists. Changes to this transform alter how Static Search Lists are processed and inserted into the system.
            - [Asset Group Import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-asset-grp-import.md) -- The Qualys Asset Group Appliance Transform map is used to transform Qualys Asset Group data to create scanner appliance records. Changes to this transform alter how scanner appliances are created and modified.
            - [Appliance Import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-appliance-import.md) -- The Qualys Appliance Transform map is used to transform Qualys Appliance data into appliance records. This is used to update the appliance records that would initially be created from the Asset Group Import. Changes to this transform alter how appliance records are updated with appliance details.
          - [REST messages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/qualys-rest-messages.md) -- Qualys REST messages are used to make calls to the Qualys API.
          - [Split Qualys detections based on vulnerability instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/split-qualys-detections.md) -- Vulnerability Response allows you to split detections from Qualys scanners, creating a unique Vulnerable Item (VIT) for each detected vulnerability instance. This supports more precise assignment to remediation teams and improves vulnerability management and tracking.
        - [Understanding the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/r7-vuln-integration.md) -- The ServiceNow Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration uses data imported from the Rapid7 data warehouse or the Rapid7 InsightVM products to help you determine the impact and priority of potentially malicious threats.
          - [Preparing for the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/r7-prerequisites.md) -- A successful integration requires planning and careful execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration assumes that you are familiar with and run Rapid7 data warehouse or Rapid7 InsightVM product scans in your environment.
            - [Set up for the Rapid7 data warehouse Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/r7-setup-dw.md) -- The Rapid7 data warehouse Integration is one of the integration types that is included with the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration.
            - [Set up for the Rapid7 InsightVM Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/r7-setup-ivm.md) -- The Rapid7 InsightVM Integration is one of the integration types that is included with the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration.
          - [Install the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/install-and-configure-r7.md) -- After you complete the set up steps for the integration so that it properly integrates with Vulnerability Response, get entitlements, download, and install the application on your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
            - [Configure the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/r7-config.md) -- Configure the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration after the application is installed and activated on your ServiceNow AI Platform.
            - [Filtering by Rapid7 sites](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/filtering-by-site.md) -- Filter by site for Rapid7 InsightVM integration types as well as Rapid7 Nexpose data warehouse integration types.
              - [Prepend SITE to your Rapid7 InsightVM site tags](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/prepend-site.md) -- Add the prefix, SITE, to your Rapid7 InsightVM site tags to highlight them among your list of tags.
            - [Deduplicate Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration data warehouse records](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/dedup-data-warehouse.md) -- When migrating to the InsightVM integration type from the Data Warehouse integration type, you can deduplicate existing data warehouse vulnerable items as long as they belong to the same source data as your Rapid7 InsightVM data.
          - [Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration run status chart](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/r7-integr-run-status.md) -- Rapid7 Nexpose sensors collect the data and automatically send it to the Rapid7 Nexpose or Rapid7 InsightVM products, which continuously analyze and correlates the information.
            - [View the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration import runs status dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/check-imports-rapid7.md) -- Use the Rapid7 vulnerability import run status dashboard to verify the success of your integration runs, locate any issues and inform your remediation decisions.
          - [Initiate rescan for the Rapid7 Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-r7-rescan.md) -- Initiate rescans in the Rapid7 platform to verify that your vulnerable items have been remediated between scheduled scanning cycles.
          - [Set additional filter parameters for Rapid7 InsightVM imports](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/r7-add-filters.md) -- Customize the filtering parameters for your scheduled queries with the Rapid7 Integration for Security Operations to help you further refine the vulnerability data you import with the Rapid7 InsightVM product.
        - [Understanding the Shodan Exploit Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/shodan-exploit-vuln-integration.md) -- The ServiceNow Shodan Exploit Integration application uses data imported from the Shodan search engine to help you determine the impact and priority of potentially malicious exploits.
          - [Preparing for the Shodan Exploit Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/shodan-exploit-prerequisites.md) -- A successful Shodan Exploit Integration requires planning and careful execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The Shodan Exploit Integration assumes that you are familiar with the Shodan search engine and API.
          - [Install and configure the Shodan Exploit Integration for Security Operations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/install-shodan-and-configure.md) -- Before you can run the Shodan Exploit Integration in your instance, download and install it from the ServiceNow Store. This application is available as a separate subscription.
            - [Set Shodan Exploit Integration import time](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/set-import-time-shodan.md) -- For your convenience, you can reset the start time for the Shodan exploit integrations.
            - [Perform a manual Shodan exploit import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/manual-shodan-import.md) -- If your initial import failed, or you do not want to wait for the scheduled initial import, you can perform a full data import independent of the daily scheduled job.
          - [View the Shodan Exploit Integration import run status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/check-imports-shodan.md) -- Use the Vulnerability Integration Runs related list to verify the success of your integration runs, locate any issues, and inform your remediation decisions.
        - [Understanding the Tenable Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenableIntegration.md) -- The Vulnerability Response Integration with Tenable application developed by ServiceNow engineering for the Tenable Vulnerability Integration uses data imported from the Tenable.io, Tenable.sc, and Tenable.cs products to help you prioritize and remediate vulnerabilities for your assets. The application is available with a separate subscription from the ServiceNow Store.
          - [Tenable.io integrations with the Vulnerability Response and Configuration Compliance applications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-io-integrations-list.md) -- The Tenable.io integrations in the Vulnerability Response Integration with Tenable with Tenable application are available for use with both the Vulnerability Response and Configuration Compliance applications.
          - [Tenable.sc integrations with the Vulnerability Response application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-sc-integrations-list.md) -- The Tenable.sc integrations in the Vulnerability Response Integration with Tenable application.
          - [Tenable.cs integrations with the Vulnerability Response and Container Vulnerability Response application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-cs-integrations-list.md) -- The Tenable.cs integrations in the Vulnerability Response Integration with Tenable application are available in the Vulnerability Response and Container Vulnerability Response applications.
          - [Preparing for the Tenable Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-setup-checklist.md) -- Prepare for the ServiceNow Tenable Vulnerability integration by performing the following setup tasks.
          - [Install the Vulnerability Response Integration with Tenable application using Setup Assistant](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenableInstall.md) -- Before you run the ServiceNow Tenable Vulnerability Integration, you need to download the Vulnerability Response Integration with Tenable application from the ServiceNow Store and install and configure it using the Setup Assistant in the Vulnerability Response application.
          - [Data retrieval settings for the Tenable Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-data-rerieve.md) -- The following data retrieval settings help you determine specifically the type and scope of data you want to import from the ServiceNow Tenable Vulnerability Integration to your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [REST messages for the Tenable Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-rest-msgs.md) -- The ServiceNow Tenable Vulnerability Integration REST messages are used to make calls to the Tenable API.
            - [Data transformation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-data-transformref.md) -- After you identify the data to import, it’s retrieved from the Tenable product and processed through a set of data sources and transforms in your instance.
          - [Set import filters](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-add-filters.md) -- Customize the filtering parameters for your scheduled queries with the Tenable Vulnerability Integration to help you further refine the vulnerability data you import with the Tenable.io product.
          - [Tenable Vulnerability Integration reporting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-integration-reporting.md) -- View reports about vulnerabilities and vulnerable items on the Vulnerability Response dashboards.
            - [Integration run status chart for the Tenable Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-integration-run.md) -- The Tenable Integration Run Status module provides you with a graphical view of the status of Tenable integration runs.
          - [Initiate rescan for the Tenable.sc integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-tenable-rescan.md) -- Verify your vulnerable items have been remediated between scheduled scanning cycles by initiating rescans in the Tenable platform. You can initiate a rescan on-demand for vulnerable items for the Tenable.sc product from your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [Initiate rescan for the Tenable.io integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-tenable-rescan-tenable-io.md) -- Verify that your vulnerable items have been remediated between scheduled scanning cycles by initiating rescans in the Tenable platform. You can initiate a rescan on-demand for vulnerable items for the Tenable.io product from your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [Update configuration items with the network partition identifier for the Tenable Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-updateCI-NPI.md) -- Create unique configuration items (CIs) for assets in your environment that share IP addresses. Identify the distinct assets across your environment and automatically update the CIs on your existing discovered item, vulnerable item, and detection records to give you more details about your vulnerabilities.
          - [Import modifications for the Tenable Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-optional-vul-modify.md) -- Configure optional modifications and streamline some of the data specifically for the Tenable Vulnerabilities import integration in the ServiceNow Tenable Vulnerability Integration.
          - [Split Tenable detections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/tenable-split-detections.md) -- ServiceNow Vulnerability Response enables the splitting of detections from Tenable scanners, enabling for the creation of a unique vulnerable item (VIT) for each detected vulnerability instance. This split enables the assignment of VITs to various remediation teams, enhancing the management and tracking of vulnerabilities.
          - [Configure Test Result Granularity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cc-tenable-tr-granularity.md) -- Configure the Tenable test result granularity to ensure the system imports results at a more detailed level based on your selected configuration keys. In addition to the default keys, you can configure additional keys based on your requirements to increase granularity. Granular imports allow teams to manage their respective assets independently and prevent data from being overwritten when multiple records share common identifiers.
          - [Compliance test uniqueness key](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cc-tenable-compliance-test-uniqueness-key.md) -- The Tenable compliance test uniqueness key determines how the system identifies incoming compliance test records during ingestion and whether they are treated as new records or updates to existing ones.
            - [Configure compliance test uniqueness key](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cc-tenable-config-test-uniqueness-key.md) -- Configure the uniqueness key for Tenable compliance tests to control how the system identifies configuration test records during ingestion and prevent records from being overwritten when multiple tests share the same control identifier.
        - [Understanding the Atlassian Jira integration with Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/jira-vuln-integration.md) -- The Atlassian Jira integration with Vulnerability Response enables you to leverage different issue-tracking and agile management tools for tracking efforts of vulnerability remediation.
          - [Preparing for the Jira Vulnerability Response integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/jira-prerequisites.md) -- A successful Jira Vulnerability integration requires planning and the execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The Jira Vulnerability Response integration assumes that you are familiar with the Jira product and API.
          - [Install the Vulnerability Response Integration with Jira](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/jira-install.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the Jira product properly integrates with Application Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
          - [Configure rules in the agile tool configuration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/jira-configure.md) -- Jira issues are created on the Jira platform if the records created in the Application Vulnerable Item, Container Vulnerable Item, Application Remediation Task and Container Remediation Task match the rules created in the agile tool configuration.
            - [Create agile issue manually using list action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-agile-issue-manually.md) -- When a list action: Create Jira Issue is triggered, records that match the rule configuration criteria should be considered for “Create Jira Issue” and those records should be ingested into the sn_vul_agile_tool_manifest table with the mapping selected in the configuration rule.
            - [Create agile issue manually using form action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-agile-issues-form-action.md) -- Create an agile issue manually using form action. If the record is matched with any configuration rule condition, then a pop-up box will be populated with values that are selected in the configuration rule and if it does not match any criteria then the user will be allowed to select values for pre-defined fields.
          - [Manually synchronize the status of the Jira issue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/sync-jira-issue-status-servicenow-manually.md) -- Update the state of the single record by clicking on the Sync Agile Status button of the form context menu and update the states of multiple records by clicking the list action Sync Agile Status of the Application Vulnerable Item, Container Vulnerable Item, and Remediation Task table in the Vulnerability Manager Workspace.
            - [Configure scheduler to create issues automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/configure-scheduler-create-issues-automatically.md) -- Configure the scheduler to run and automatically create the Jira issues for the records that have been created in the sn_vul_agile_tool_manifest table.
            - [Configure scheduler to update issues automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/configure-scheduler-update-issues-automatically.md) -- Configure the scheduler to update the Jira issues automatically for the records that have been added in the sn_vul_agile_tool_manifest table.
            - [Configure scheduler to synchronize the Jira status to ServiceNow automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/config-scheduler-sync-jira-status-vr-auto.md) -- Configure the scheduler to synchronize the state of Jira issues with application vulnerability item, container vulnerability items and tasks in the sn_vul_agile_manifest table.
        - [Vulnerability Response Integration with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Cloud Compute integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-pcc-integration.md) -- The Prisma Cloud Compute integration enables you to scan hosts to detect vulnerabilities.
          - [Preparing for the Vulnerability Response Integration with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Cloud Compute](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-prisma-prep-checklist.md) -- You can prepare for the Vulnerability Response Integration with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Cloud Compute by performing setup tasks.
          - [Install the Vulnerability Response Integration with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Cloud Compute](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-install-prisma-cloud-compute.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, complete the installation and configuration steps so that the Prisma Cloud Compute product properly integrates with Vulnerability Response and Container Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
          - [Configure the Vulnerability Response Integration with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Cloud Compute application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-prisma-install-and-configure.md) -- Install and configure the Vulnerability Response Integration with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Cloud Compute application to import data from Prisma Cloud Compute. You can use the imported data to prioritize and remediate vulnerabilities for your hosts.
          - [Integration run status chart for Prisma Cloud Compute Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-prisma-integration-run.md) -- The Prisma Cloud Compute Integration Run Status module provides you with a graphical view of the status of Prisma Cloud Compute Integration runs.
        - [Wiz Vulnerability Response Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-wiz-exploring-host-cf.md) -- Import vulnerability and compliance data from Wiz scanners into your ServiceNow AI Platform instance to help you get deeper insights into your cloud infrastructure risks. These integrations provide you with a comprehensive assessment of your overall cloud security posture and help you drive remediation actions directly from your instance.
          - [Install](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-wiz-host-vuln-install.md) -- Activate the required application.
          - [Configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-config-wiz-host-vuln.md) -- Configure the integration in your Wiz service account and assign roles in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
            - [Activate the Wiz Asset Integration and identify resource types for import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-assets-resources-tab.md) -- Activate the Wiz Asset Integration and identify only the resource types (assets) with this integration that you want to import.
            - [Filter host vulnerabilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-host-tab-filters.md) -- Set the filtering values to import the host vulnerability data that you want.
            - [Filter container vulnerabilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-container-tab-filters.md) -- Set the filtering values to import the container vulnerability data that you want.
            - [Filter test results](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-test-result-tab-filters.md) -- Set the filtering values to import the cloud test results data that you want.
            - [Filter issues](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-issues-tab-filters.md) -- Set the filtering values to import Wiz Issues that identify assets involved in toxic combinations of vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
            - [Filter host test results](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-host-test-result-tab-filters.md) -- Set the filtering values to import Wiz host test results.
            - [Configure the Application List, SCA and Secret integrations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/avr-wiz-config.md) -- Configure the Wiz Application Vulnerabilities, SCA, and Secrets Integrations.
            - [Backfill integrations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-backfill.md) -- Prior to v1.1, the Wiz Backfill integrations import and process details for your Assets IDs that are flagged as missing by the primary vulnerability integrations. Starting with v1.1, the backfill integrations are deprecated.
            - [Use](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-activate-backfill.md) -- You can schedule the integration import times on the their records on the Vulnerability Integrations [sn_vul_int_fw_integration] table and launch them on-demand.
          - [Field mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/wiz-vul-resp-integration-view-findings.md) -- Review source and target fields and view imported data on tables and records in your ServiceNow Now Platform AI instance.
        - [Manually create a vulnerability integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_VulnerabilityIntegrations.md) -- Vulnerability integrations provide the ability for customers and vendors to enrich the vulnerability data on their instance by retrieving data from external systems and vendors. This ability can simplify the vulnerability remediation life cycle by keeping the instance synchronized with other vulnerability management systems.
          - [Define a new vulnerability integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/t_DefineNewIntegration.md) -- A vulnerability integration pulls report data from a third-party system, generally to retrieve vulnerability data, and process that reporting data using data sources or a custom processor.
            - [Vulnerability integration script](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_AboutVulnIntegScript.md) -- On the Vulnerability Integration form, the integration script is a reference to a script include that extends the VulnerabilityIntegrationBase script include.
              - [Single call integrations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_SingleCallIntegrations.md) -- Single call (single page) integration scripts are the simplest types of integrations. They require one call, often to an external source of data, to retrieve data. Only retrieveData() is required to be implemented for single page/single call integrations.
              - [Multiple call integrations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_MultipleCallIntegrations.md) -- Multiple call (or multiple page) integration scripts are a bit more complicated. They require multiple calls to a data source to retrieve data.
              - [Attachments as retrieveData() return values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_AttachmentsAsReturnValues.md) -- Sometimes, it is preferable be return an attachment from retrieveData(). The logic to create and/or retrieve an attachment is implementation-specific, but after the attachment is known, its information can be returned.
            - [Report processor strategies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_AboutRptProcStrategies.md) -- The Report processor strategy field on the Vulnerability Integration form is used to select the method to process the data returned by the vulnerability integration script when the vulnerability integration process is executed.
              - [Use the data source attachment report processor strategy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/t_DataSrcAttachStrategy.md) -- The Data Source Attachment report processor strategy is used to pass data retrieved by the integration script to configured data sources.
              - [About custom report processor scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_AboutCustomRptProcScript.md) -- On the Vulnerability Integration form, the Report processor is a reference to a script include that extends the VulnerabilityReportProcessorBase script include. The functionality contained in this script is called by the VulnerabilityIntegrationController and defines the means by which the data returned by the integration script are processed.
            - [Integration factory script fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_AboutFactoryScript.md) -- The Vulnerability Integration form contains the integration factory script and, when the Custom Report Processor report processor strategy is selected, the Processor factory script. These fields are used to provide the logic to actually instantiate the object defined by the script include reference fields, Integration script, and Report processor, respectively.
          - [Manually run a vulnerability integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/t_ManualRunVulnInteg.md) -- Vulnerability integrations for Vulnerability Response and Application Vulnerability Response are configured to run on a scheduled basis. However, you can run them manually when needed.
        - [Manual ingestion of vulnerabilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/manually-ingest-vulnerabilities.md) -- Manually ingest vulnerabilities into the Vulnerability Response application so that you can remediate them quickly without having to wait for scanner results.
          - [Ingest vulnerabilities manually](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ingest-vulnerabilities-manually.md) -- Manually ingest vulnerabilities by uploading a template with vulnerabilities data.
          - [Verify manual integration run](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/verify-manual-integration-run.md) -- Verify that the integration run is successful. View the number of individual detections and the vulnerable items (VIs) created or updated due to the integration run.
          - [Configure auto-close manual detections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/configure-auto-close-manual-detections.md) -- Enable the auto-close manual detections to close stale manual detections automatically.
          - [Verify upload status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/verify-upload-status.md) -- Verify whether the vulnerability integration run was successful, partially successful, or a failure.
          - [Template for manual ingestion of vulnerabilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/manual-ingestion-template.md) -- The template provides columns that must be filled with vulnerability and configuration item (CI) data. After the template is populated and uploaded, the data is processed.
      - [Vulnerability Response remediation overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/cj-common-vuln-tasks.md) -- Vulnerability Response remediation is a phased process consisting of verifying import completion, triaging new vulnerabilities, and monitoring progress to completion. Approached in this way, remediation becomes manageable, timely, and in many ways, automated.
        - [Verify successful completion of Vulnerability Response integration imports](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-integr-run-status.md) -- The easiest way to determine whether your imports have succeeded is to use an Integration Run Status dashboard. The Integration Run Status dashboard provides an example of a graphical view of the status of third-party integration runs.
        - [View Vulnerability Response vulnerable item detection data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr_view_detection.md) -- The complete data gathered by your third-party scanner integrations with Vulnerability Response are displayed on the Detections and Initial Detections tabs on the vulnerable item records (VIT). It is also displayed on Detection records on the Vulnerable Item Detection list in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [Verify Vulnerability Response vulnerable item detection data on integration run (VINTRUN) records](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr_host_integration_run.md) -- From integration run records in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance, you can locate third-party integration vulnerable item detection data based on the date and time of scans. Verify the scan successfully completed, view the number (counts) of individual detections, as well as any vulnerable items (VIs) that are created or updated directly as a result of the scans.
        - [Viewing patch data and scheduling patches in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-patch-orch-view-data.md) -- Starting with v16.1 of Vulnerability Response, you can schedule patches and view patch data along with solution and other vulnerability information on records in both the classic environment and the Vulnerability Response Workspaces in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [Patch data and state rollup for patch orchestration in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-patch-rollup-data.md) -- Starting with v16.1 of Vulnerability Response patch data and states are rolled up to Patch Update and other records in the Vulnerability Response application.
          - [View patches without solutions in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-patch-view-no-solution.md) -- Starting with v16.0 of Vulnerability Response, for patches that have no solutions after an import, the system then searches for patch IDs in the vulnerability reference data so that you can view these patches on vulnerability records.
          - [Viewing patch orchestration data on the Vulnerability Response dashboards](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-patch-orch-dbs.md) -- Starting with v16.1, you can view patch update data in the classic environment on the dashboards that are included with the Vulnerability Response application.
        - [View a solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-solutions.md) -- View all solutions, solutions with highest-supersedence and solutions associated with vulnerable items to inform your remediation activities.
        - [Create a vulnerability solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-vul-solution.md) -- Create a vulnerability solution so that you can track vulnerability solutions that are not covered by third-party solution content.
          - [Manually exclude solutions from third-party records or vice versa](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/exclude-solutions.md) -- Exclude the relationship between a third-party vulnerability and a solution because it is not relevant to the CI you are remediating, or it is not a concern in your environment. Manually exclude solutions using either a third-party vulnerability or solution record.
        - [Triage vulnerabilities automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vuln-automated-triage.md) -- Reviewing and triaging new vulnerabilities is necessary to ensure successful remediation. Transform vulnerability imports into remediation tasks with automated vulnerable item (VI) assignment, risk calculation, remediation targets, and VI grouping.
          - [Edit vulnerable items in bulk in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/bulk-edit-vis.md) -- Edit vulnerable items in bulk to save time by selecting fields and running an asynchronous bulk edit job in the background.
          - [Ungrouped Vulnerability Response vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ungrouped-vul-items.md) -- Vulnerable items are automatically assigned to a remediation task when they are imported. However, sometimes, if a vulnerable item (VI) is created manually, for example, the VI can become an orphan. The Ungrouped vulnerable item module lists those vulnerable items without a group.
            - [View ungrouped Vulnerability Response vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-ungrouped-vul-items.md) -- Vulnerable items that are not assigned to a group are placed in a viewable list.
            - [Manually add a vulnerable item to a remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/add-vul-item-to-group.md) -- If a vulnerable item is left ungrouped, you can add it to a remediation task.
          - [Remove assignments from vulnerable items and remediation tasks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-unassign-vi.md) -- Clear the Assigned to and Assignment group fields on vulnerable item records in the Vulnerability Response, Application Vulnerability Response, and Container Vulnerability Response applications.
          - [Approve or reject an unassign request in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-approve-unassign-requests.md) -- Approve requests for the removal of vulnerable items from an assignment group or an individual user in the Vulnerability Response application.
          - [Automatic closing of vulnerable items and detections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/auto-close-common.md) -- You can automatically close stale detections and vulnerable items (VIs) associated with retired CIs using the Auto-Close rules module.
            - [Working with retired configuration items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/working-with-retired-CIs.md) -- Decommissioned configuration items (CIs) are moved to retired, archived, or deleted state in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB). Vulnerability Response contains vulnerable items (VIs) that are made up of CIs. When the state of a CI is updated to retired, the associated VIs are closed with the substate 'CI decommissioned'.
            - [Automatically close vulnerable items related to retired CIs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/auto-close-vis.md) -- If the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) changes the life cycle stage status of a configuration item (CI) to retired, you can choose to automatically close the associated vulnerable items (VIs) and detections.
