          - [Closing stale detections in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-setup-autoclose-detections.md) -- The Auto-Close Stale Detections module helps you automatically clean up older, stale vulnerable detections not recently found by your third-party integrations. Moving these detections to Closed reduces the number of active vulnerable items and remediation tasks in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance and helps you reconcile assets in your CMDB.
            - [Automatically close stale detections in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-autoclose-detections.md) -- Enable Auto-Close Stale Detections to automatically close stale vulnerable detections not recently found by your third-party integrations.
            - [Create auto-close rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-auto-close-rules.md) -- Use auto-close rules to close older detections automatically based on the filter conditions that you set.
          - [Reclassify unclassed hardware](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/reclassify-unclassed-hardware.md) -- Reclassify unclassed hardware by updating the CI lookup rules.
        - [Manually create a remediation task in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-vulnerability-group.md) -- Creating a remediation task manually is done when you want to group vulnerable items by something other than the Remediation Task Rules criteria. For example, you can create tasks for a particular manager, or for active, new exploits, such as ransomware that include different vulnerabilities. You can also use it to group ungrouped vulnerable items.
          - [Add users to the Vulnerability Response group](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/t_SetUpVulnGroup.md) -- When the Close/Defer feature is used to defer or close a vulnerable item without requiring a scan, the Vulnerability Response group is notified to approve or reject the request. You can assign the appropriate users.
        - [Manage individual vulnerable items manually](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_IndivVulnItemMgmt.md) -- Vulnerable items represent one configuration item (CI) with a given vulnerability. Vulnerable items are imported from third-party sources, or using the SAM NVD information (link) to compare vulnerability entries to software records retrieved from the Software Asset Management module.
          - [Create Vulnerability Response vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/t_CreateVulnerableItems.md) -- Multiple methods create vulnerable items (VI). Most commonly, an integration to a vulnerability scanner is installed and configured to import results nightly. There are cases, like physical security vulnerabilities, when you might prefer to manually add vulnerable item records.
            - [Defer a vulnerable item](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/defer-vul-item.md) -- If you determine that the issue associated with a vulnerable item (VIT) is of low risk and can be immediately deferred without further analysis, you can use the Defer feature.
              - [Request an extension for a deferred vulnerable item](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/extend-defer-vul-item.md) -- Request an extension for a deferred vulnerable item before it reaches its deferred until due date. As a remediation owner, you’re no longer required to wait until the deferred due date to make this request.
          - [Refresh Vulnerability Response vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/refresh-vuln-items.md) -- The Update status related link is used to have vulnerable items inspected to see if there are any additional vulnerable items that belong to a remediation task. Use it if an update is warranted outside the scheduled job.
            - [Identify and escalate security issues in third-party software](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/escalate-sec-issue-3rdparty.md) -- You can view software vulnerabilities returned from third-party entries to determine remediation. Use this information to match the vulnerable software entries to a Software Asset Management discovery model.
            - [Identify and escalate security issues using NVD](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/escalate-sec-issue-NVD.md) -- When Common Vulnerability and Exposures identifier (CVE-ID) records are downloaded from the National Vulnerability Database (NVD), they are compared to the software in your company network as identified by the Software Asset discovery model. When a CVE-ID matches vulnerable software or configuration item in your network, a vulnerable item is created. You use the information in the CVE-ID record to decide whether to escalate the vulnerable item for remediation.
            - [Identify and escalate security issues using CWE](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/escalate-sec-issue-CWE.md) -- View the library of Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) records from the National Vulnerability Database (NVD) to understand how they relate to the Common Vulnerability and Exposure (CVE) records. Then use this information to match the vulnerable software entries to a Software Asset Management discovery model.
            - [View the remediation target status of a Vulnerability Response vulnerable item](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-remed-target-task.md) -- When a vulnerable item has nearly reached (or passed) its remediation target date, as defined by a remediation target rule, the vulnerable item record is updated with a status. This information can help the analyst proactively monitor upcoming remediation activities.
        - [Working with unmatched CIs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/working-unmatched-cis.md) -- As a Vulnerability Manager and Analyst, you can view and reclassify unmatched Configuration Items (CIs), reconcile unmatched discovered items, reapply CI lookup rules on the selected discovered items, and de-duplicate existing CIs.
          - [View and reclassify unmatched configuration items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-discovered-items.md) -- Configuration items (CIs) that are not found in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) are placed in a viewable list of discovered items. This list offers a convenient way to reclassify unmatched CIs.
          - [Reconcile unmatched discovered items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/reapply-reconcile-unmatched-discovered-items.md) -- Create a scheduled job to reconcile unmatched discovered items.
            - [Reapply CI lookup rules on selected discovered items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/reapply-ci-lookup-rules-di.md) -- Reapply the configuration item (CI) lookup rules on selected discovered items from the discovered item list view select actions. If the CI changes after you reapply the rules, the discovered items are updated with the new CI and impacted detections. Vulnerable items are also updated.
            - [Reapply CI Lookup Rules Enhancements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/reapply-ci-lookup-rules-enhancements.md) -- Reapply the configuration item (CI) lookup rules on selected discovered items like os and netbios.
          - [Steps to help prevent duplicate or orphaned records after running Vulnerability Response CI lookup rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ci-identifier-rules-impl-test.md) -- Take steps to help prevent duplicate or orphan records resulting from matching (configuration items (CIs) within the CMDB.
          - [De-duplicating existing configuration items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/reapply-deduplication.md) -- Whenever configuration items (CIs) are updated through a deduplication task, the discovered items (DIs) that are related to those CIs are also updated. The vulnerable items (VIs) and detections are also updated with the CI.
          - [Resolve remediation tasks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/remediate-vuln-groups.md) -- The flexibility inherent in Vulnerability Response allows you to remediate vulnerabilities in whatever way suits your security organization.
            - [Defer a Remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/defer-vuln-group.md) -- If you determine that the issue associated with a remediation task (VUL) is a low priority and can be immediately deferred without further analysis, you can use the Request Exception feature.
              - [Request an extension for a deferred remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/extend-defer-vuln-group.md) -- Request an extension for a deferred remediation task (VUL) before it reaches its deferred until due date. As a remediation owner, you’re no longer required to wait until the deferred due date to make this request.
            - [Close a remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/close-vuln-group.md) -- If you determine that the issue associated with a remediation task can be immediately closed without further analysis, you can use the Close feature. Starting with v23.0 of Vulnerability Response, the Close button has been removed for a remediation task and the closure of a remediation task is driven by the scanner.
        - [Identifying duplicate vulnerable items from multiple scanners](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/correlate-vis-from-diff-scanners.md) -- If you are using multiple scanners on the same asset to detect vulnerabilities, multiple vulnerable items (VIs) might be created. You can identify these duplicate VIs to ensure that the duplicate vulnerabilities are not assigned to the remediation owners.
          - [Automatically resolve duplicate vulnerabilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/automate-workflows-for-duplicate-vulnerabilities.md) -- Starting from Vulnerability Response (VR) v17.1, you can automatically resolve any duplicate vulnerabilities on an asset.
        - [Create and edit a classification group](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-edit-classification-group.md) -- Create groups so that the underlying rules of this group can classify the table records based on the conditions. The rules can be used to select any field in the corresponding table.
          - [Create and edit a classification rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-classification-rule.md) -- Create rules to automatically classify the incoming vulnerabilities to the correct teams for remediation.
          - [Apply a rule to an existing vulnerability](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/apply-rule-existing-vulnerability.md) -- When you change a classification rule, rerun all the active rules on all the vulnerability entries. You can also retrofit the existing vulnerabilities, by applying the newly created classification rules to the existing vulnerabilities.
          - [Deactivate or delete a classification rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/delete-vulnerability-classification-rule.md) -- Deactivate or delete a classification rule if it is no longer needed or relevant.
        - [Create an exclusion rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-exclusion-rule.md) -- Create a rule to filter or exclude detections from getting converted into vulnerable items (VITs) during ingestion.
        - [Change Management tasks for Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vuln_chg_mgmnt_tasks.md) -- From remediation tasks (RTs), create change requests, associate RTs to existing change requests, or split remediation tasks into new tasks with subsets of vulnerable items (VI).
          - [Create a change request from a remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vuln-change_mgmnt_create_change.md) -- As an IT remediation owner, create a change request (CHG) directly from a remediation task (VUL) for all the vulnerable items in the group. Create a change request with pre-populated information that includes the preferred solution to expedite your investigation for vulnerabilities that require manual intervention.
          - [Associate a remediation task to an existing change request](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vuln_change_mgmnt_associate_chng.md) -- As an IT remediation owner, avoid creating duplicate change requests (CHG) as you work to resolve your remediation tasks by associating a remediation task (VUL) to a change request that is already available in your instance.
          - [Split a remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vuln_change_mgmnt_split.md) -- As an IT remediation owner, from an existing remediation task (VUL), identify a subset of vulnerable items (VI) that you want to move to a new group.
          - [State synchronization between change requests and remediation tasks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vuln_change_mgmnt_state_synch.md) -- There is a synchronized relationship between the State fields of remediation tasks (VULs) and the State fields of change requests (CHG) in the Vulnerability Response product. As a change request moves through its life cycle, it also moves the state of any related remediation tasks automatically.
        - [Assess your exposure to vulnerable software](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr_sw_expsure_task.md) -- You can provide the publisher and product information in the Exposure Assessment module to assess your zero-day (current day) exposure of your assets to vulnerable software using the ITSM Software Asset Management (SAM) Foundation application.
        - [Viewing assignment recommendations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ml_view_assignment_recommendations.md) -- An assignment group is usually allocated to a vulnerable item by using assignment rules. These rules are configured by the vulnerability administrator to allocate the correct assignment groups that are based on the data received from third-party scanners.
          - [Request assignment group recommendations for a vulnerable item](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ml-assignment-rec-for-vi.md) -- Request Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations so that you can view assignment group recommendations. You can request recommendations if a matching assignment group is not available or is incorrect for a vulnerable item (VI).
          - [Request assignment group recommendations for multiple vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ml-request-bulk-edit-vis.md) -- Use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to view the assignment group recommendations for multiple vulnerable items (VIs) using a bulk edit. When multiple VIs are selected, a prediction is performed on a random sample of the selected VIs.
          - [Request assignment group recommendations for a remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ml-request-bulk-edit-vg.md) -- Use Vulnerability Assignment Recommendations to view assignment group recommendations for a remediation task (VUL).
        - [Requesting and approving an exception](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/request-approve-exception.md) -- You can request to defer the remediation of a vulnerable item or remediation task for a specified period. For example, as a remediation owner, you can request an exception if a patch is not available for a machine. Approvers who have access can approve requests from other users.
          - [Request an exception for a vulnerable item](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/raise-exception-vi.md) -- Request an exception for a vulnerable item (VI) that can’t be remediated immediately. For example, as a remediation owner, you can request an exception if a patch isn’t available for a machine.
          - [Request an exception for a remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/raise-exception-vg.md) -- Request an exception to defer a remediation task for a specified period if it can’t be resolved immediately.
          - [Request a bulk exception](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/bulk-edit-ex-mgmt.md) -- Use the bulk edit option to request an exception for multiple vulnerable items (VITs) instead of manually selecting each item.
          - [Request an exception using GRC: Policy and Compliance Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/integration-with-grc.md) -- Request policy exceptions using the GRC policy exception management capability in the Policy and Compliance Management application from within Vulnerability Response.
            - [Request a bulk exception using GRC: Policy and Compliance Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/bulk-edit-grc.md) -- Select a group of vulnerable items and submit a bulk exception.
          - [Approve or reject an exception request in Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/approve-ex-request.md) -- Approve or reject the exception requests for vulnerable items or remediation tasks that can't be remediated immediately. You must assess these requests for risk and then approve them for deferral until they can be remediated.
        - [Working with an exception rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/working-with-exception-rule.md) -- You can request an exception for vulnerabilities, vulnerable items (VIs), or configuration items (CIs) that can't be remediated or deferred immediately. By automating the VI deferral process, you can defer the matching VIs based on the rule when the system identifies them.
          - [Create an exception rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-exception-rule.md) -- Create a rule to automatically request an exception for a specific condition for a group of vulnerable items (VIs), such as a rule with a condition that is based on the vulnerability severity of these VIs. With this rule, you can defer new and existing VIs automatically if they match the approved rule condition.
          - [Approve an exception rule request](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/approve-ex-rule-request.md) -- Assess exception rule requests from users so that you can approve or reject these requests.
          - [Activating an exception rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/activating-ex-rule.md) -- A rule is activated on its "Valid from" date. After activation, it automates the exception process for vulnerable items (VIs).
          - [Request an extension for an exception rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/extend-ex-rule-request.md) -- Request an extension for a deferred exception rule before it reaches its deferred until due date. As a remediation owner, you’re no longer required to wait until the deferred due date to make this request.
          - [Reopen an exception rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/reopen-ex-rule.md) -- Reopen an exception rule that has been rejected, but you want to resubmit. Reopening the rule moves it to the Draft state.
          - [Update an approved exception rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/update-approved-ex-rule.md) -- Cancel an approved rule to be able to update it. For example, before you can modify any dates or add a condition to an approved rule, you must cancel it so that the remediation task (VUL) is deleted, and the vulnerable items (VIs) move to the Open state.
          - [Delete an exception rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/delete-ex-rule.md) -- Delete an exception rule that is not required anymore. For example, you can delete a rule if you don't want to defer a vulnerable item (VI) during ingestion.
        - [Marking and approving a false positive](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/request-approve-fp.md) -- Vulnerable items (VIs) and remediation tasks (VULs) can be marked as false positives. Approvers with write access can approve such requests from other users.
          - [Mark as a false positive](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/raise-fp-request.md) -- Mark a vulnerable item (VI) or remediation task (VUL) as a false positive if the warning given by the scanner is not actually an issue. For example, if a configuration item has been decommissioned but the scanner is still raising an issue related to it, mark it as a false positive.
            - [Bulk edit for false positive](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/bulk-edit-fp.md) -- Use bulk edit to mark multiple vulnerable items (VITs) as false positive. If multiple VITs are selected, a remediation task is formed with these items.
          - [Approve or reject a false positive](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/approve-fp-request.md) -- As a false positive approver, you can approve or reject false positive requests from other users.
      - [Analytics and Reporting Solutions for Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/monitor-vuln-rem-prog.md) -- Monitoring vulnerability remediation involves viewing trends, managing risk, and monitoring assignment groups. You can review high risk issues, assignment group workloads, deferrals and, reoccurring vulnerabilities. Vulnerability Response offers tools, reports, and procedures to make that process more productive and efficient.
        - [Using the default Vulnerability Response dashboards](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_VulnHomepage.md) -- The Vulnerability Response overview dashboard (Vulnerability Management) provides an executive view into vulnerabilities and vulnerable items, helping the Vulnerability Admin pinpoint areas of concern quickly. The Vulnerability Response remediation dashboard (Vulnerability Remediation) allows a remediation specialist to focus on the remediation tasks and vulnerable items they own.
        - [Platform Analytics Solutions for Vulnerability Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vulnerability-mgmnt-content-pack.md) -- Platform Analytics Solutions contain prepackaged Performance Analytics and Reporting content for use with other ServiceNow AI Platform products. Finish the following sentence with a short statement on the business use case for this Solution. For example: This Analytics and Reporting Solution helps you track trends in incidents and plan your incident management strategy going forward.This Analytics and Reporting Solution permits you to track the volume, performance and progress of vulnerabilities from initial analysis and detection to containment, or remediation. You can filter reports by assignment group, exploits, risk rating, or state.
          - [Vulnerability Management CISO dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vulnerability-mgmnt-CISO-dashboard.md) -- With the Vulnerability Management CISO dashboard, view data such as Key Performance Metrics (KPIs) for vulnerability remediation, see the highest risks, verify scan coverage, and learn how to lower risks.
            - [Configure the Scan Coverage reports](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ciso-add-ci-class-to-scan-coverage-report.md) -- Enable your users to run scan coverage reports from the CISO dashboard. With these reports, your users can see what the organization's scan coverage is over a period of time.
            - [Configure the PA indicators for the CISO Dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ciso-view-indicators.md) -- Monitor the automated and formulated CISO indicators so that you can manage the vulnerability data in your instance.
          - [SecOps Vulnerability Response Health dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/health-dashboard-overview.md) -- The Vulnerability Response Health dashboard is a tool designed to empower organizations with comprehensive insights into the implementation and usage of their Vulnerability Response applications.
            - [Modifying the threshold values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/health-dashboard-threshold-val.md) -- You can modify the threshold values for the SecOps VR Health dashboard data based on your preferences.
        - [Vulnerability Management (PA) dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vulnerability-mgmnt-pa-dashboard.md) -- Track the volume, performance and progress of vulnerabilities from initial analysis and detection to containment, or remediation. You can filter reports by assignment group, exploits, risk rating, or state, for example. Quickly gain insight into your vulnerability exposure and which business services are affected.
          - [View Performance Analytics for Vulnerability Response [PA] reports in real-time](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr_real_time_report_PA_VR.md) -- To manage your most important vulnerable items (VIs) and help you remediate them quickly, view reports on-demand, in real-time on the Vulnerability Management (PA) dashboard. The Performance Analytics for Vulnerability Response application is available with a separate subscription from the ServiceNow Store.
          - [View the Performance Analytics indicators for Vulnerability Response [PA]](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/view-pa-indicators.md) -- View automated and formulated Performance Analytics indicators representing the vulnerability data in your instance.
        - [Aggregated reports framework](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vulnerability-response-common-content-pack.md) -- Using aggregated reports framework, you can create reports for the data that involves complex and long-running on-the-fly queries for better performance.Finish the following sentence with a short statement on the business use case for this Solution. For example: This Analytics and Reporting Solution helps you track trends in incidents and plan your incident management strategy going forward.
          - [Configure an aggregation for source data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/configure-indicator-for-source-data.md) -- To analyze the performance of a specific facet in a business process that is recorded in a ServiceNow table, create an aggregation specific to the facet and create a report for this aggregation.
            - [Run multiple aggregations simultaneously](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/run-multiple-aggregations.md) -- You can schedule the execution of multiple aggregations using a single Aggregation schedule.
            - [Configure the number of aggregations that can run simultaneously](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-config-arf-processor.md) -- A new processor or a background job, Aggregated Reporting Framework Processor is shipped with the base system, which executes the scheduled jobs that are related to the Aggregated Reports Framework table. This enables the entire subsystem to run in the background, and it allows better utilization of system resources when they’re available.
          - [Create a report using an aggregation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/generate-report.md) -- To visualize and analyze the data specific to an aggregation that you created, generate a report by providing a condition for the aggregation in the condition builder.
      - [Vulnerability Response reference information](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-reference-info.md) -- The following topics provide you with links, reference information, troubleshooting, and knowledge articles, in addition to field data on the forms and records found in Vulnerability Response.
        - [Implementation checklist for the Vulnerability Response application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-implement-chklist.md) -- This checklist lists the steps required for a basic implementation of the Vulnerability Response application on your ServiceNow AI Platform instance. When you have completed these tasks, the base system is ready for operation and verification.
        - [Vulnerability Response remediation task states](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-rt-states.md) -- Third-party integrations import vulnerable item detection data that create new vulnerability items (VITs) or update existing VITs. Detection states update VIT states in so far as they’re Open or Closed.
          - [Detections, remediation tasks, and vulnerable item states](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-detections-rt-vi-states.md) -- Third-party integrations retrieve the vulnerable item detection data. Detections are distinct occurrences of vulnerabilities as reported by the scanners.
          - [Remediation tasks and vulnerable item states](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-rt-vi-states.md) -- Remediation tasks and vulnerable items states can affect each other. Most of the time, a remediation task state updates the vulnerable item state, with the highest precedence task state used to update the vulnerable items in the group.
          - [State roll-up and roll-down scenarios](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-state-rollup-rolldown.md) -- State roll-up and roll-down scenarios automatically sync the status of remediation tasks (RTs) and vulnerable items (VITs), ensuring real-time updates across both. This dynamic interaction reduces manual tracking, enhances accuracy, and provides users with an up-to-date view of progress, making vulnerability management more efficient and helping users make informed decisions quickly.
          - [Remediation task state for Vulnerable Items (VITs) in multiple groups](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-rt-state-for-multi-grp-vi.md) -- When a VIT is in multiple remediation tasks, (RT in the following tables), and its own state has not been set, the higher precedence group state determines the state of that VIT, as shown in the following table.
        - [Additional settings for domain separation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/ds-additional-config.md) -- For precise execution and results, the following precautions and settings are required for domain separation in Vulnerability Response, Application Vulnerability Response, Container Vulnerability Response, and Configuration Compliance.
        - [Discovered Items form fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/discovered-items-fields.md) -- Assets are automatically matched to configuration items (CIs) in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) when they’re imported using the CI Lookup Rules. Records for unmatched CIs are automatically created during third-party vulnerability integration imports and included in Discovered Items. However, for cloud assets that aren’t present in CMDB Cl, the record is created in the cloud resource class.
        - [Vulnerability Response vulnerability form fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vulnerability-fields.md) -- Vulnerabilities are created automatically when records are downloaded from the National Vulnerability Database (NVD), Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE), or third-party integrations and stored under Libraries in Vulnerability Response.
        - [Remediation target rule fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/sem-rt-rules-fields.md) -- Use remediation target rules to define how remediation timelines are calculated and maintained for findings. Administrators can configure base target dates and recalculation behavior when risk ratings change. The following table describes all fields available in the remediation target rule form.
        - [Remediation task form fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vulnerability-group-fields.md) -- Using remediation tasks, the analyst can monitor progress and drive the mediation process more efficiently.
        - [Questionnaire Configuration form fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/compensating-controls-approval-rule-new-record-form.md) -- You can define distinct questionnaire for a distinct collection of vulnerabilities or remediation tasks by filtering the vulnerabilities or remediation tasks respectively in the VR Questionnaire Configuration section of Settings for VR Exception Management form.
        - [Vulnerability Response vulnerable item form fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vulnerable-item-fields.md) -- Vulnerable items are automatically created during third-party vulnerability integration imports.
        - [Solution form fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/solution-fields.md) -- Solutions are created automatically when records are downloaded from third-party solution integrations and stored under Solutions in Vulnerability Response.
        - [Approval workflow configurations for unassign request](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vr-unassign-approval-rules.md) -- You can design the approval workflow for the removal of assignments from vulnerable items (VIs, VITs), remediation tasks, application vulnerable items (AVITs), and container vulnerable items (CVITs) for you and your group.
        - [Vulnerability Response remediation task rule examples](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vul-group-rules-examples-v10.md) -- Examples of remediation task rules using vulnerability and risk, or impact, or configuration item (CI).
        - [Risk score calculation example for Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/vuln-calc-risk-rule-example.md) -- You can determine the risk score calculators to generate risk scores that use the vulnerability and asset data unique to your organization.
        - [Error handling for detections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/detections-error-handling.md) -- Errors are logged while creating detections, or creating and updating vulnerable items, while processing the attachments. If a vulnerability integration process fails, errors are logged in the 'Vulnerability Integrations Log' tab.
      - [Mobile experience for Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mobile-vr.md) -- As a remediation owner, you can access the Vulnerability Response (VR) application on your ServiceNow AI Platform instance with your Android or iOS mobile device.When decided, copy all mobile to Madrid for June. Make a list of where it gets referenced in existing docs for weaving it in by mention. ("Action available in the mobile app.")
        - [Set up checklist for the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mobile-vr-setupinstll-mobile.md) -- The following checklist includes the set up tasks that you are required to complete in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance and on your mobile device prior to using the Vulnerability Response Mobile app.
        - [Log in to the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mobile-vr-login-mobileapp.md) -- Open the Vulnerability Response Mobile app and add a ServiceNow AI Platform instance with Vulnerability Response to your mobile device.
        - [View, assign, and edit remediation tasks with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mobile-reassign-vg.md) -- View, assign, or edit the fields on remediation task (RT) records assigned to your group. Navigate through vulnerabilities, vulnerability items, and solutions records and related lists to view solutions, deployment metrics, and items to address.
        - [View, reassign, and edit remediation tasks assigned to you with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mobile-vr-yourvg-vis.md) -- View, assign, or edit the fields on remediation tasks records that are assigned to you. Navigate through records and related lists to view solutions, deployment metrics, and items to address.
        - [Search for remediation tasks with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mobile-vr-search-vgs.md) -- Search for remediation tasks that match specific search criteria that you select.
        - [Filter records with the Vulnerability Response Mobile app](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/mobile-filter-records.md) -- Set additional filters to limit the number of records that are displayed on a screen. Filtering records in the mobile app works like filtering with a condition builder on the ServiceNow AI Platform.
      - [Vulnerability Response Orchestration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/c_VulnRespOrchestration.md) -- With Vulnerability Response Orchestration workflows and activities you can interact with and retrieve data from Windows or UNIX-based systems and environments using workflow orchestration. By enriching data, you can shorten the remediation life cycle.
        - [Scan vulnerability workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/scan-vulnerability-workflow.md) -- The Vulnerability Response Scan Vulnerability workflow rescans a remediation task.
        - [Scan vulnerability item workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/scan-vulnerable-item-workflow.md) -- The Vulnerable Response Scan Vulnerability Item workflow rescans a vulnerable item.
        - [Variables for Create Scan Record for Vulnerabilities activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/vulnerability-response/create-scan-record-for-vuln-activity.md) -- Vulnerability scans for single or multiple vulnerable items can be run using the Create Scan Record for Vulnerabilities workflow activity included in the base system. When the input is passed to the activity, it creates a scan record.
    - [Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-landing.md) -- The Application Vulnerability Response (AVR) feature of the ServiceNow Vulnerability Response application imports application vulnerable items (AVITs) and, according to rules, allows you to remediate application vulnerabilities. Vulnerability Response is available by separate subscription.
      - [Explore](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/app-vuln-mgmt.md) -- Application vulnerabilities are vulnerabilities on your custom software applications that are scanned throughout the application’s development life cycle.
      - [Configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-configuring.md) -- Before you run Application Vulnerability Response in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance, you must configure it.
        - [Configure Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/configure-avm.md) -- Do the following setup steps prior to configuration so that you can ensure that your configuration is complete.
          - [Verify that the scheduled job for updating CWE records is running](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-verify-CWE-sched-job.md) -- Use Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) records downloaded from the CWE database for reference when deciding whether a vulnerability must be escalated. Update common weakness records from the Common Weakness Enumeration database on a regularly scheduled basis. You can also update the default script or write your own scripts, as needed.
          - [Verify that the scheduled job for updating NVD records is running](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-verify-NVD-sched-job.md) -- Identify the repositories that you want updated regularly. You can execute a scheduled job to update National Vulnerability Database (NVD) records on a nightly or weekly basis. If it is not already running, you can enable the job
          - [Install](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-activate-integrations.md) -- Enable Application Vulnerability Response integrations to perform regular updates.
          - [Define Application Vulnerability Response email notifications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-define-email-notifications.md) -- Set up email notifications to share useful information about important updates and activities such as approval and rejection of false-positive requests. Creating an email notification involves specifying when to send it, who receives it, and what it contains.
            - [Create or edit remediation target notifications in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-create-remediation-notification.md) -- Vulnerability administrators can edit the remediation target notification or add new ones, specifying when to send the notification, who receives the notification, and what content is in the notification.
          - [Exception Management in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-exception-management.md) -- When your organization can't comply with a published vulnerability management or security policy, standard, or guideline, you can request an exception. Exception management entails requesting, reviewing, approving, or rejecting exceptions to an application vulnerable item (AVIT) that cannot be remediated according to the policy.
            - [Configure Exception Management for Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/configure-exception-management-application-vulnerability-response.md) -- Limit the duration of an exception requested and add a questionnaire to the exception or false positive request using the module. By default, an exception is requested using the ServiceNow Application Vulnerability Response module. You can also request an exception using the GRC: Policy and Compliance Management integration.
            - [Configure approval rules for Exception Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-exception-mgt-approval-rules.md) -- Starting with Application Vulnerability Response v15.0, use the flow designer to approve exception requests for exception management, exception rules, and false positive management. If you are deploying Application Vulnerability Response (AVR) for the first time, the flow designer is enabled by default.
            - [Deferring remediation in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/aws-defer-awaiting-implementation.md) -- Starting with v20.0, you can defer remediation with the Awaiting Implementation state that is available for application vulnerable items (AVI)s and remediation tasks as they move through their life cycles. You can only transition records to this state manually by selecting Awaiting Implementation from AVI and remediation task records in the Under Investigation state.
            - [Add an exception approver for Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-add-exception-approver.md) -- Add users to the approver groups so that you can request an exception.
            - [Requesting and approving an exception in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-request-approve-exception.md) -- You can request to defer the remediation of an application vulnerable item and a remediation task for a specified period.
              - [Request an exception for an application vulnerable item](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-raise-exception.md) -- Request an exception for an application vulnerable item that cannot be remediated immediately. For example, as a developer, you can request an exception if a patch is not available for a machine.
              - [Request an exception for application vulnerabilities using GRC: Policy and Compliance Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/integrate-with-grc-avr.md) -- Request policy exceptions using the GRC policy exception management capability in the Policy and Compliance Management application from within Application Vulnerability Response.
              - [Request an exception for an application remediation task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/raise-exception-art.md) -- Request an exception to defer a application remediation task for a specified period if it can’t be resolved immediately.
              - [Approve exception rules and exception rule extension requests in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-approve-ex-request.md) -- Approve exception requests for application vulnerable items that can't be remediated immediately. You must assess these requests for risk and then approve them for deferral until they can be remediated.
              - [Define policy reason mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/define-policy-reason-mapping.md) -- You can define the reason choices to be available to any user who requests an exception.
              - [Request an extension for an exception rule in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/extend-ex-rule-request-avr.md) -- Request an extension for a deferred exception rule before it reaches its deferred until due date. As a remediation owner, you’re no longer required to wait until the deferred due date to make this request.
              - [Request an extension for a deferred remediation task in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/extend-defer-app-vuln-group.md) -- Request an extension for a deferred remediation task (VUL) before it reaches its deferred until due date. As a remediation owner, you’re no longer required to wait until the deferred due date to make this request.
              - [Request an extension for a deferred application vulnerable item in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/extend-defer-app-vul-item.md) -- Request an extension for a deferred application vulnerable item before it reaches its deferred until due date. As a remediation owner, you’re no longer required to wait until the deferred due date to make this request.
            - [Create, delete, and cancel an exception rule for Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-create-exception-rule.md) -- Create a rule to request an exception automatically for application vulnerable items (AVI)s that meet specific conditions.
          - [Application Vulnerability Response remediation tasks and task rules overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-rt-rt-rules-overview.md) -- Configure remediation tasks (AVULs) to help analysts and remediation specialists organize application vulnerable items (AVI) and analyze them in bulk. The criteria by which remediation tasks are formed is configured so that AVIs are automatically assigned into remediation tasks. Using remediation tasks, you can monitor progress and drive the remediation process more efficiently.
            - [Create, edit, and delete Application Vulnerability Response remediation task rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-create-rt-rule.md) -- You can create rules to automatically group application vulnerable items (AVI) into remediation tasks (AVUL) based on filter conditions. These rules automatically group AVIs as they're imported or manually created.
            - [Create an application remediation task manually in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-create-rt-manually.md) -- Create an application remediation task (AVUL) from another application remediation task record and add application vulnerable items (AVITs) manually.
            - [Create auto-close rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-create-auto-close-rules.md) -- Use auto-close rules to close older AVITs automatically based on the filter conditions that you set.
        - [Configure penetration testing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/pen-test-config-v16-1.md) -- You can configure the sprint duration and estimated effort for penetration testing assessment types. This provides the scheduling functionality for application owners, helping them determine a tentative time frame for their penetration test assessment requests.
          - [Configure sprints for penetration testing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/pen-test-config-sprints.md) -- Configure the sprint duration and capacity for the ethical hacking team so they can manage the sprint capacity for penetration test assessments.
          - [Configure assessment types for penetration testing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/pen-test-config-assessment-types.md) -- Configure the estimated effort for each type of penetration testing assessment. This enables you to manage the capacity of each sprint, by estimating the effort required for each assessment type.
      - [Integrate with other applications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-integrations.md) -- Vulnerability Response includes support for third-party integrations.
        - [Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/blackduck-integration.md) -- The Vulnerability Response integration with Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck uses the data that is imported from the Black Duck Software Composition Analysis (SCA) tool to help you determine the impact and priority of the flaws in your code.
          - [Prepare pre-integration tasks for Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/blackduck-prerequisites.md) -- Plan and execute the pre-integration tasks for the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck so that your integration is successful. The Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck assumes that you’re familiar with the Black Duck Software Composition Analysis (SCA) application and APIs.
          - [Install the ServiceNow Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/install-blackduck.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, you must complete the installation and configuration steps so that the Black Duck Software Composition Analysis (SCA) tool properly integrates with Application Vulnerability Response.
          - [Configure the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/configure-blackduck.md) -- Before you run the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck on your instance, you must complete the installation and configuration steps so that the Black Duck Software Composition Analysis (SCA) tool properly integrates with the Application Vulnerability Response application.
            - [View the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck import run status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/blackduck-vuln-check-imports.md) -- Verify the success of your integration runs, locate any issues, and confirm your remediation decisions by viewing the Vulnerability Integration Runs related list.
          - [Data transformation for the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/blackduck-data-transformref.md) -- After you identify the data that you want to import, the data is retrieved from the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck, processed through a set of data sources, and transformed in your instance.
          - [Import the project information from the Black Duck integration instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/blackduck-integration-activities.md) -- Import the project information from the Black Duck integration instance to make sure that the vulnerability integrations run sequentially. A successful integration helps you to identify and ingest the application vulnerable items (AVIs).
            - [Import Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck project versions to the Discovered Applications table](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/blackduck-integration-activity-application-list-integrtn.md) -- Import the Vulnerability Response integration with Black Duck project versions to the Discovered Applications [sn_vul_release] table so that the Black Duck Vulnerability integrations run sequentially. A successful integration helps you to identify and ingest the application vulnerable items (AVIs).
            - [Import the application vulnerable items from the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/blackduck-integration-activity-application-vulnerable-item.md) -- Import the application vulnerable items (AVIs) from the Vulnerability Response integration with Black Duck so that the integration runs sequentially. A successful integration helps you to identify and ingest the AVIs.
            - [Set the import times for the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/set-import-time-blackduck.md) -- Set the start time for the Vulnerability Response Integration with Black Duck imports. You can set the integrations ahead of time so that you get better performance and faster import runs.
            - [Include Closed Black Duck application vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/include-closed-avits-blackduck.md) -- Include Closed application vulnerable items (AVIs) during the Black Duck vulnerable item integration imports because by default, they aren’t created during the integration imports. If you want to create them, you must change the system property.
        - [Fortify Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/fortify-vuln-integration.md) -- The Fortify Vulnerability Integration uses data imported from the Fortify product to help you determine the impact and priority of flaws in your code.
          - [Preparing for the Fortify Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/fortify-prerequisites.md) -- A successful Fortify Vulnerability Integration requires planning and the execution of pre-integration tasks. Prepare for the integration by performing these tasks. The Fortify Vulnerability Integration assumes that you are familiar with the Fortify product and API.
          - [Install the ServiceNow Vulnerability Response Integration with Fortify](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/install-fortify.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the Fortify product properly integrates with Application Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
          - [Configure the Fortify Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/configure-fortify.md) -- Before you run the integration on your instance, the installation and configuration steps must be completed so the Fortify product properly integrates with the Application Vulnerability Response feature of Vulnerability Response. This application is available as a separate subscription.
          - [View the Fortify Vulnerability Integration import run status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/fortify-check-imports.md) -- Use the Vulnerability Integration Runs related list to verify the success of your integration runs, locate any issues, and inform your remediation decisions.
          - [Fortify Vulnerability Integration modification and activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/fortify-optional-modifications.md) -- Configure optional modifications specifically for the Fortify Vulnerability Integration.
            - [Perform a manual Fortify application vulnerability import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/manual-fortify-import.md) -- If your initial import failed, or you do not want to wait for the scheduled initial import, you can perform a full data import independent of the daily scheduled job.
            - [Import data using the Fortify Vulnerability Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/set-import-time-fortify.md) -- Import data from the Fortify scanner, to map Application Vulnerable Items (AVIs), thereby enriching the data in your instance.
            - [Include Closed Fortify on Demand application vulnerable items](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/include-closed-avits-fortify.md) -- By default, Closed application vulnerable items (AVITs) are not created during Fortify Vulnerable Item integration imports. If you want to create them, you must change a system property.

        - [Wiz Vulnerability Response Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/wiz-exploring-avr-sca-secrets.md) -- Import application vulnerability response data that includes application, Software Composition Analysis (SCA) and secrets data with the Wiz Application Vulnerability Response Integration.
        - [Manual ingestion of vulnerabilities for Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/manual-ingestion-of-vulnerabilities-for-application-vulnerability-response.md) -- Security professionals and application testers can create and manage the application penetration test findings within the Penetration Testing Workspace.
          - [Upload application vulnerabilities using a template file](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/ingest-vulnerabilities-manually-avr.md) -- Use a template file to upload the application vulnerabilities. To access and download the template, navigate to All Manual AVIT Ingestion Upload File UI .
          - [Verify integration run](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/verify-manual-integration-run-avr.md) -- If the integration run is successful, review the count of individual detections and the penetration test findings created or updated.
          - [Verify upload status](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/verify-upload-status-avr.md) -- Verify whether the vulnerability integration run was successful, partially successful, or a failure.
        - [Tenable Web Application Scanning Vulnerability Response Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/tenable-was-integration.md) -- Import applications, findings and associated application vulnerabilities from the Tenable Web application and manage them in ServiceNow Vulnerability Response.
          - [Configure Tenable Web Application Scanning Vulnerability Response Integration using Setup Assistant](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/configure-tenable-was-integration-using-setup-assistant.md) -- Establish authenticated communication between ServiceNow and Tenable.was using API keys so the platform can retrieve application and vulnerability data.
          - [Import Applications Data from Tenable Web Application Scanning Vulnerability Response Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/import-applications-data-from-tenable-was.md) -- Import application assets from Tenable WAS Vulnerability Response Integration using the Assets Export APIs and create import queue entries for transformation.
          - [Import Vulnerabilities Data from Tenable Web Application Scanning Vulnerability Response Integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/import-vulnerabilities-data-from-tenable-was.md) -- Import vulnerability findings and associated metadata using Tenable WAS Export Findings APIs.
      - [Use](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-monitor-rem-prog.md) -- Monitoring remediation is a process that begins with reviewing status and ends with closing application vulnerable items (AVITs). Application Vulnerability Response offers tools and procedures to make that process more productive and efficient.
        - [View vulnerability libraries](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-view-vuln-libraries.md) -- You can view vulnerability data imported from the National Vulnerability Database (NVD), Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE), or third-parties to decide whether to escalate a remediation task.
          - [Application Vulnerability fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-vulnerability-fields.md) -- Vulnerabilities are created automatically when records are downloaded from the National Vulnerability Database (NVD), Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) or third-party integrations. NVD and CWE are stored under Libraries in Vulnerability Response or under Vulnerabilities in Application Vulnerability Response.
        - [Identify applications in Application Vulnerability Response automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-ci-lookup-rules.md) -- When data is imported from a third-party integration, Application Vulnerability Response automatically uses application data to search for matches in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB). It does this using lookup Rules. These rules identify applications for the application vulnerable item (AVI) record to aid in remediation.Update with VR CI LOOKUP RULES changes.
          - [Create a CI lookup rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-create-ci-lookup-rules.md) -- The CI Lookup Rules module contains rules that define what fields have matching data in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB). These rules are used to identify applications and application releases and add them to the application vulnerable item (AVI) record to aid in remediation.
            - [Reapplying lookup rules in Application Vulnerability Response](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avr-reapplying-ci-lookup-rules.md) -- You can reapply your configuration item (CI) lookup rules to update existing CIs (scanned applications and product models) on discovered application records.
            - [Prevent duplicate or orphaned records after running Application Vulnerability Response lookup rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-ci-lookup-rules-impl-test.md) -- Take steps to prevent duplicate or orphan records resulting from matching (configuration items (CIs) within the CMDB.
        - [Assign application vulnerable items in Application Vulnerability Response automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-assignment-rules.md) -- Automatically assign application vulnerabilities based on application tags, or any of the assignment groups in the Configuration Item [cmdb_ci] or platform assignment groups, to reduce the mean time to assignment.
          - [Create or edit Application Vulnerability Response assignment rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-create-assign-rules.md) -- You can create rules to automatically assign application vulnerable items (AVIs) based on filter conditions. These rules assign AVIs as they are imported or manually created.
          - [Removing assignments from Application Vulnerability Response vulnerable items and remediation tasks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/unassigning-avits.md) -- You can clear the Assigned to and Assignment group fields on Application Vulnerability Response vulnerable items directly from the application vulnerable item and remediation task records that you determine might be incorrectly assigned to you or your groups.
        - [Calculate risk in Application Vulnerability Response automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-calculators-rules.md) -- Application vulnerability calculators automate calculating initial risk values for the fields on application vulnerable items (AVIs). Risk calculations offer insight into prioritizing remediation. The condition for each calculator is evaluated in order, and the first matching calculator is used.
          - [Define fields and weights for the risk rule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-vuln-calc-define-risk-rule-fields.md) -- Customize the parameters and weights for the risk rule so that you can generate risk scores that use the vulnerability and asset data that are unique to your organization. By selecting the fields that are included in the risk rule, you can define an effective risk scoring framework.
          - [Create an application vulnerability calculator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-create-calculator.md) -- An application vulnerability calculator is a pre-defined formula to calculate a target field when certain criteria are met. Calculators, which calculate the application vulnerable item (AVI) Risk Score, can contain Risk Rules. Risk calculations offer insight in prioritizing remediation.
        - [Map the severity of an application vulnerable item automatically](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-create-severity-map.md) -- Application Vulnerability Response severity mapping transforms third-party source severity fields to recognizable fields within Vulnerability Response.
          - [Filtering within Application Vulnerability Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/security-management/application-vulnerability-response/avm-filtering.md) -- Calculators, and Assignment Rules use conditions during import, created using the condition builder.
